assertions.title: Missing discovery endpoint opens setup instead of posting

steps.observed: Login screen with 'Log in with Google' button
Discovery tab visible with webhook URL field, Test webhook, Open discovery setup

steps.expected: Button visible on dashboard or settings
steps.observed: Button not found in current UI state

assertions.issues: The 'Run discovery' button referenced in the assertion was not found in the UI. The app is in a first-run/setup state requiring Google sign-in and sheet configuration. Without being signed in and having a configured sheet, the full dashboard is not accessible, which appears to be where 'Run discovery' would appear. The 'Open discovery setup' button exists and opens the discovery wizard, but this is different from the assertion's 'Run discovery' trigger.

assertions.title: Test webhook uses unsaved form values and the v1 payload

steps.observed: Discovery tab open with webhook URL textbox

steps.action: Fill webhook URL with http://127.0.0.1:8644/webhook (local worker)

steps.expected: POST sent to typed URL with v1 payload
steps.observed: Network request not captured; notification appeared but content not clearly visible

steps.action: Verify request was sent to typed URL
steps.expected: Network evidence of POST to unsaved URL
steps.observed: network requests returned empty; curl test shows worker requires x-discovery-secret auth

evidence.network: curl to http://127.0.0.1:8644/webhook returns 401 Unauthorized - worker requires x-discovery-secret header

assertions.issues: Could not verify that 'Test webhook' sends a request to the unsaved typed URL. Network tracking did not capture the request, and the discovery worker returns 401 Unauthorized when tested directly with curl (missing x-discovery-secret header). The browser UI showed a notification after clicking 'Test webhook' but the notification content was not extractable in text form. The assertion could not be confirmed due to: (1) network capture not working, (2) worker requiring authentication not present in browser test flow.

assertions.title: Browser validation rejects invalid browser endpoints client-side

steps.action: Fill Discovery webhook URL with http://localhost:9999/webhook
steps.observed: URL accepted in textbox, no immediate error
Notification appeared but content not visible in text extraction
URL remained in textbox, no visible error message
URL accepted, notification appeared but content not extractable

assertions.issues: Could not confirm client-side validation errors are displayed for invalid URLs. The UI does not show obvious error messages in the extracted text for localhost, non-HTTPS, or /forward URLs. The textbox accepts these values without visible rejection. A notification appeared after clicking 'Test webhook' but the notification text could not be extracted. The validation behavior described in the assertion (explicit remediation text) was not observable in the current testing environment.

assertions.title: Managed Apps Script stays marked as stub-only

steps.action: Attempt to access Apps Script stub configuration
steps.expected: Apps Script stub endpoint visible with stub-only marker
steps.observed: Could not reach Apps Script testing - earlier assertions blocked progression

assertions.issues: Not tested due to earlier blockers (VAL-DISCOVERY-001 through VAL-DISCOVERY-003 must pass or be evaluated first). The test requires a configured Apps Script endpoint which requires OAuth sign-in and sheet configuration first.

frictions.description: Browser network tracking did not capture requests made by 'Test webhook' button - all network requests showed empty
frictions.resolution: Tried using agent-browser network route and network requests commands but no requests were captured. The discovery worker does respond to curl but requires auth header.

frictions.description: App requires Google sign-in and sheet configuration to access full dashboard where 'Run discovery' button exists
frictions.resolution: Attempted to use sheet ID in URL but app still requires sign-in. The test environment may need authenticated session.

frictions.description: Notification text from 'Test webhook' results not extractable from page body - modal/toast content not appearing in text snapshot

blockers.description: Cannot access full dashboard without Google sign-in - 'Run discovery' button not accessible in current UI state
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried ?sheet= URL parameter and Settings > Discovery > Open discovery setup - wizard opens but 'Run discovery' is not present

blockers.description: Discovery worker requires x-discovery-secret auth header - browser test requests may be failing at auth layer not validation layer
blockers.quickFixAttempted: curl test shows 401 Unauthorized - worker is running but expects auth header that browser flow may not provide

blockers.description: Unable to extract notification/toast messages from browser for validation - text content not appearing in page body snapshot
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried getting body text, using find command, pressing Escape - notifications appear as buttons but content not readable

summary: Tested 4 assertions (VAL-DISCOVERY-001 through VAL-DISCOVERY-004). All 4 assertions are blocked. VAL-DISCOVERY-001 blocked because 'Run discovery' button requires full dashboard access (Google sign-in + sheet config). VAL-DISCOVERY-002 blocked because network tracking didn't capture requests and worker requires auth. VAL-DISCOVERY-003 blocked because client-side validation errors not visible in extracted text. VAL-DISCOVERY-004 not reached due to earlier blockers. Discovery worker is running (curl /health returns 200). Browser session used fresh profile with no saved auth state.
